root_text.an10.20:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā kurūsu viharati kammāsadhammaṁ nāma kurūnaṁ nigamo.
root_text.an10.20:2.1: “Dasayime, bhikkhave, ariyāvāsā, ye ariyā āvasiṁsu vā āvasanti vā āvasissanti vā.
root_text.an10.20:2.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu pañcaṅgavippahīno hoti, chaḷaṅgasamannāgato, ekārakkho, caturāpasseno, paṇunnapaccekasacco, samavayasaṭṭhesano, anāvilasaṅkappo, passaddhakāyasaṅkhāro, suvimuttacitto, suvimuttapañño.
root_text.an10.20:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno kāmacchando pahīno hoti, byāpādo pahīno hoti, thinamiddhaṁ pahīnaṁ hoti, uddhaccakukkuccaṁ pahīnaṁ hoti, vicikicchā pahīnā hoti.
root_text.an10.20:3.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu pañcaṅgavippahīno hoti.
root_text.an10.20:4.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu cakkhunā rūpaṁ disvā neva sumano hoti na dummano, upekkhako viharati sato sampajāno.
root_text.an10.20:4.7: manasā dhammaṁ viññāya neva sumano hoti na dummano, upekkhako viharati sato sampajāno.
root_text.an10.20:4.8: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu chaḷaṅgasamannāgato hoti.
root_text.an10.20:5.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu satārakkhena cetasā samannāgato hoti.
root_text.an10.20:5.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu ekārakkho hoti.
root_text.an10.20:6.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu saṅkhāyekaṁ paṭisevati, saṅkhāyekaṁ adhivāseti, saṅkhāyekaṁ parivajjeti, saṅkhāyekaṁ vinodeti.
root_text.an10.20:6.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu caturāpasseno hoti.
root_text.an10.20:7.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno yāni tāni puthusamaṇabrāhmaṇānaṁ puthupaccekasaccāni, seyyathidaṁ:
root_text.an10.20:7.3: ‘sassato loko’ti vā, ‘asassato loko’ti vā, ‘antavā loko’ti vā, ‘anantavā loko’ti vā, ‘taṁ jīvaṁ taṁ sarīran’ti vā, ‘aññaṁ jīvaṁ aññaṁ sarīran’ti vā, ‘hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti vā, ‘na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti vā, ‘hoti ca na ca hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti vā, ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti vā,
root_text.an10.20:7.4: sabbāni tāni nunnāni honti paṇunnāni cattāni vantāni muttāni pahīnāni paṭinissaṭṭhāni.
root_text.an10.20:7.5: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu paṇunnapaccekasacco hoti.
root_text.an10.20:8.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno kāmesanā pahīnā hoti, bhavesanā pahīnā hoti, brahmacariyesanā paṭippassaddhā.
root_text.an10.20:8.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu samavayasaṭṭhesano hoti.
root_text.an10.20:9.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno kāmasaṅkappo pahīno hoti, byāpādasaṅkappo pahīno hoti, vihiṁsāsaṅkappo pahīno hoti.
root_text.an10.20:9.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu anāvilasaṅkappo hoti.
root_text.an10.20:10.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sukhassa ca pahānā dukkhassa ca pahānā pubbeva somanassadomanassānaṁ atthaṅgamā adukkhamasukhaṁ upekkhāsatipārisuddhiṁ catutthaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an10.20:10.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu passaddhakāyasaṅkhāro hoti.
root_text.an10.20:11.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno rāgā cittaṁ vimuttaṁ hoti, dosā cittaṁ vimuttaṁ hoti, mohā cittaṁ vimuttaṁ hoti.
root_text.an10.20:11.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu suvimuttacitto hoti.
root_text.an10.20:12.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu ‘rāgo me pahīno ucchinnamūlo tālāvatthukato anabhāvaṅkato āyatiṁ anuppādadhammo’ti pajānāti, doso me pahīno …pe… ‘moho me pahīno ucchinnamūlo tālāvatthukato anabhāvaṅkato āyatiṁ anuppādadhammo’ti pajānāti.
root_text.an10.20:12.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu suvimuttapañño hoti.
root_text.an10.20:13.1: Ye hi keci, bhikkhave, atītamaddhānaṁ ariyā ariyāvāse āvasiṁsu, sabbe te imeva dasa ariyāvāse āvasiṁsu;
root_text.an10.20:13.2: ye hi keci, bhikkhave, anāgatamaddhānaṁ ariyā ariyāvāse āvasissanti, sabbe te imeva dasa ariyāvāse āvasissanti;
root_text.an10.20:13.3: ye hi keci, bhikkhave, etarahi ariyā ariyāvāse āvasanti, sabbe te imeva dasa ariyāvāse āvasanti.
root_text.an10.20:13.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, dasa ariyāvāsā, ye ariyā āvasiṁsu vā āvasanti vā āvasissanti vā”ti.

translation_text.an10.20:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying in the land of the Kurus, near the Kuru town named Kammāsadamma.
translation_text.an10.20:1.2: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants:
translation_text.an10.20:2.1: “There are these ten abodes of the noble ones in which the noble ones of the past, present, and future abide.
translation_text.an10.20:2.3: A mendicant has given up five factors, possesses six factors, has a single guard, has four supports, has cast aside idiosyncratic interpretations of the truth, has totally given up searching, has unsullied intentions, has stilled the physical process, and is well freed in mind and well freed by wisdom.
translation_text.an10.20:3.1: And how has a mendicant given up five factors?
translation_text.an10.20:3.2: It’s when a mendicant has given up sensual desire, ill will, dullness and drowsiness, restlessness and remorse, and doubt.
translation_text.an10.20:3.3: That’s how a mendicant has given up five factors.
translation_text.an10.20:4.1: And how does a mendicant possess six factors?
translation_text.an10.20:4.2: It’s when a mendicant, seeing a sight with their eyes, is neither happy nor sad. They remain equanimous, mindful and aware.
translation_text.an10.20:4.3: Hearing a sound with their ears …
translation_text.an10.20:4.4: Smelling an odor with their nose …
translation_text.an10.20:4.5: Tasting a flavor with their tongue …
translation_text.an10.20:4.6: Feeling a touch with their body …
translation_text.an10.20:4.7: Knowing an idea with their mind, they’re neither happy nor sad. They remain equanimous, mindful and aware.
translation_text.an10.20:4.8: That’s how a mendicant possesses six factors.
translation_text.an10.20:5.1: And how does a mendicant have a single guard?
translation_text.an10.20:5.2: It’s when a mendicant’s heart is guarded by mindfulness.
translation_text.an10.20:5.3: That’s how a mendicant has a single guard.
translation_text.an10.20:6.1: And how does a mendicant have four supports?
translation_text.an10.20:6.2: After appraisal, a mendicant uses some things, endures some things, avoids some things, and gets rid of some things.
translation_text.an10.20:6.3: That’s how a mendicant has four supports.
translation_text.an10.20:7.1: And how has a mendicant cast aside idiosyncratic interpretations of the truth?
translation_text.an10.20:7.2: Different ascetics and brahmins have different idiosyncratic interpretations of the truth. For example:
translation_text.an10.20:7.3: the cosmos is eternal, or not eternal, or finite, or infinite; the soul and the body are one and the same, or the soul is one thing, the body another; after death, a realized one still exists, or no longer exists, or both still exists and no longer exists, or neither still exists nor no longer exists.
translation_text.an10.20:7.4: A mendicant has cast out, cast aside, thrown out, discarded, let go of, given up, and relinquished all these.
translation_text.an10.20:7.5: That’s how a mendicant has cast aside idiosyncratic interpretations of the truth.
translation_text.an10.20:8.1: And how has a mendicant totally given up searching?
translation_text.an10.20:8.2: It’s when they’ve given up searching for sensual pleasures, for continued existence, and for a spiritual life.
translation_text.an10.20:8.3: That’s how a mendicant has totally given up searching.
translation_text.an10.20:9.1: And how does a mendicant have unsullied intentions?
translation_text.an10.20:9.2: It’s when a mendicant has given up intentions of sensuality, malice, and cruelty.
translation_text.an10.20:9.3: That’s how a mendicant has unsullied intentions.
translation_text.an10.20:10.1: And how has a mendicant stilled the physical process?
translation_text.an10.20:10.2: It’s when, with the giving up of pleasure and pain and the disappearance of former happiness and sadness, they enter and remain in the fourth absorption, without pleasure or pain, with pure equanimity and mindfulness.
translation_text.an10.20:10.3: That’s how a mendicant has stilled the physical process.
translation_text.an10.20:11.1: And how is a mendicant well freed in mind?
translation_text.an10.20:11.2: It’s when a mendicant’s mind is freed from greed, hate, and delusion.
translation_text.an10.20:11.3: That’s how a mendicant is well freed in mind.
translation_text.an10.20:12.1: And how is a mendicant well freed by wisdom?
translation_text.an10.20:12.2: It’s when a mendicant understands: ‘I’ve given up greed, hate, and delusion, cut them off at the root, made them like a palm stump, obliterated them, so they’re unable to arise in the future.’
translation_text.an10.20:12.3: That’s how a mendicant’s mind is well freed by wisdom.
translation_text.an10.20:13.1: Mendicants, whether in the past, future, or present, all noble ones abide in these same ten abodes of the noble ones.
translation_text.an10.20:13.4: These are the ten abodes of the noble ones in which the noble ones of the past, present, and future abide.”

variant_text.an10.20:7.4: nunnāni honti paṇunnāni → nuṇṇāni honti panuṇṇāni (bj, sya-all, pts1ed)
